goal: Make ESData classify and operate empty tables deterministically across local, VPS, and GitHub without fake compliance data.

userStories.title: Classify every table by source behavior
acceptanceCriteria: Every empty table is classified as workflow-empty, allowed-empty, blocker, or derived-blocker
The classification distinguishes official scraped data from operational internal tables

userStories.title: Close local-to-VPS drift for P0 populated-local tables
acceptanceCriteria: P0 tables populated locally are populated on the VPS or have a documented upstream blocker
No fixture, sample, community, or LLM-generated data is used to satisfy production counts
VPS evidence includes exact row counts from Postgres
userStories.verification: Run exact count SQL locally and on deploy-postgres-1, then compare P0 tables.

userStories.title: Expose table/domain availability to MCP/API
acceptanceCriteria: MCP/API responses can report populated, operational-empty, or unavailable status by domain
Unavailable domains produce explicit uncertainty instead of inferred answers
Tool metadata includes source/freshness warning when relevant
userStories.verification: Focused API/MCP tests for available and unavailable domains.

userStories.title: Generate freshness and lineage after ingestion
acceptanceCriteria: source_freshness_snapshot is populated after official-source worker runs
data_lineage links derived rows to source records or sync_log runs
Stale domains surface warnings rather than silent answers
userStories.verification: SQL counts plus source freshness endpoint/MCP metadata tests.

userStories.title: Synchronize local, VPS, and GitHub state
acceptanceCriteria: Plan and registry updates are committed and pushed to GitHub
VPS checkout has the same revision or documented drift
docs/master-execution-roadmap.md points to the active table action plan
userStories.verification: git status, git rev-parse HEAD locally and on VPS, and GitHub push result.
